title: bromopsis inermis var. hirta
intro: Bromopsis inermis var. hirta, also known as a type of wild grass or sedge, is a plant species that belongs to the grass family Poaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Bromopsis inermis var. hirta include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its dried roots and rhizomes are used in various traditional remedies.
